narrative.summary:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Atlantic Ave at Henry St — the former Colonie space, now reborn.

A seasonal, chef-driven New American restaurant with natural wine ambitions, opened February 2026 in the Brooklyn Heights space that housed Colonie for over a decade.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Atlantic Ave at Henry St — the edge where Brooklyn Heights meets Cobble Hill.

You walk into a room of wood and brick that feels lived-in, warm, like someone's been cooking here forever. Chef-owners Brendan Kelley (Aska, Per Se) and Daniel Grossman (Gage & Tollner, Foul Witch) moved here from Industry City in February 2026, and the space fits them like it was always theirs. Next door at 129 Atlantic, their all-day bakery and pizza shop Lou & Bev's is coming soon.

blocks.title: Space
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗦𝗣𝗔𝗖𝗘

Exposed brick, warm wood, the kind of neighborhood restaurant where the staff remembers your name by the third visit. Intimate without being cramped. The bar is worth sitting at if you're solo — watch the cocktails being built while Lucy Saintcyr's wine picks line the shelf behind.

blocks.title: Drinks
blocks.body:
𝗗𝗥𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦

🍷 All-natural wine list — 75+ bottles, 10 by the glass. Old World-leaning, curated by wine director Lucy Saintcyr.

🍸 Cocktails built on classic templates with local spirits and seasonal ingredients:
• M&M Negroni — mezcal, amaro, vermouth, blood orange ⭐
• Seasonal rotating cocktails

The wine program is the real draw — ask Lucy what's open and interesting.

blocks.title: Food
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗨

Contemporary American with a focus on dry-aged fish and meats. Menu changes seasonally.

𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀
• Trout Mousse ⭐
• Seasonal crudo

𝗣𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗮𝘀 (new since the Brooklyn Heights move)
• Cavatelli — white rabbit ragu, horseradish, fiore sardo ⭐
• Spaghetti al Funghi — mixed mushrooms, garlic, serrano pepper

𝗘𝗻𝘁𝗿é𝗲𝘀
• Prawn Pot Pie — made-to-order prawn bisque, seasonal vegetables ⭐
• Fish of the Day — gigante beans, mushroom garum, fermented lemon
• Rotating seasonal entrées with dry-aged meats

𝗗𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘁𝘀 (pastry chef Mariah Neston, ex-Le Rock)
• Malted Mille Feuille — chocolate crêpe dentelles, hazelnut crémeux, coffee diplomat cream ⭐
• Banoffee Sundae — banana ice cream, butterscotch toffee, buttered brioche crumbs

Note: Menu evolves seasonally. Expect new pasta additions and rotating proteins.

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

💰 $$$

Dinner for 2: ~$120-160 (with wine)

Reservations: Via Resy at confidantnyc.com

🌐 confidantnyc.com
📸 @confidant.bk
📞 (929) 252-0205

𝗛𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗦
Wed–Sun: 5:00–10:00 PM
Mon–Tue: Closed

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦
• Early weeknight (Wed/Thu 5pm): Fewer covers, more attention from the kitchen
• Bar seats for walk-ins
• Just opened Feb 2026 — still building a following, easier to get in now

discovery.text: Eater NY — hidden cocktail bar on Atlantic Ave
